about.title: An interaction ecologist with experience across marine, estuarine and terrestrial systems.
about.bio: I am a PhD candidate in Ecology and Biodiversity Conservation at UESC, with an MSc in Environmental Sciences and Technologies from IFBA/UFSB and a BSc in Biological Sciences from UFSB. I developed a strong background in marine ecology at LECOMAR (UFSB), where I investigated sea turtle ecology and larval transport. My experience also includes ecotoxicology, with a focus on environmental contaminants. I currently conduct research at LEAC and LABINT (UESC), studying frugivory interactions with an emphasis on palms. I am particularly interested in expanding collaborations that apply my expertise in interaction ecology and conservation to projects involving avifauna.
